title: Drip vs Moosend (2025): Which Is Better?
meta_description: Compare Drip vs Moosend: pricing, features, and performance. Find the best email marketing tool for your business needs.
h1: Drip vs Moosend: Detailed Comparison for 2025
intro: Drip and Moosend are both established email marketing platforms, but they serve different business needs. Drip specializes in e-commerce automation with deep store integrations, while Moosend offers affordable, flexible solutions for small businesses and startups. Understanding their differences will help you choose the right tool for your marketing goals.

comparison_table.tool_a: E-commerce businesses with deep store integration needs
comparison_table.tool_b: Small businesses and startups wanting affordable automation

comparison_table.tool_a: E-commerce tracking, revenue attribution, SMS, onsite campaigns
comparison_table.tool_b: Unlimited emails, landing pages, A/B testing, real-time analytics

choose_a_if: You run an e-commerce business and need advanced revenue tracking and attribution
You want SMS marketing integrated directly into your email automation platform
You require deep integrations with e-commerce platforms like Shopify or WooCommerce
choose_b_if: You're a small business or startup with a limited budget
You want to start with a free plan and upgrade as you grow
You need unlimited emails and landing pages without worrying about send limits

faq.a: It depends on your needs. Drip excels for e-commerce businesses requiring advanced product tracking and revenue attribution. Moosend is better for budget-conscious teams needing flexible automation and unlimited email sends. Both have strong ratings, with Moosend slightly higher on G2 (4.7 vs 4.4).

faq.q: Which is cheaper, Drip or Moosend?
faq.a: Moosend is significantly cheaper, offering a free plan for up to 1,000 subscribers and paid plans starting at $9/month. Drip has no free tier and starts at $39/month. For small businesses, Moosend is the more affordable choice.

faq.q: Can I switch from Moosend to Drip?
faq.a: Yes, both platforms support data export and import features. Most email marketing tools allow you to export your contact lists and create migration plans. Contact each platform's support team for specific guidance on transferring your automations and settings.
